title: The Sleepy Star
content:
Up in the night sky, a little star named Pip shone as brightly as it could. All the other stars were wide awake and twinkling. But Pip had been shining since before the sun went down, and now Pip's light was starting to flicker and grow dim.

Pip blinked slowly. "I am so very sleepy," Pip said with a tiny yawn. "But the night is not over yet! What will the children below see if I go dark?" Pip tried hard to shine brighter, but it was so difficult to stay awake.

The great silver Moon drifted gently close. "Little Pip," said the Moon softly, "you have shone so beautifully tonight. It is perfectly all right to rest. Your brothers and sisters will keep the sky bright while you sleep."

Pip smiled, feeling warm and peaceful. Slowly, Pip's light softened to a gentle glow and faded into a quiet sleep. All the other stars watched over little Pip tenderly, shining on through the whole long night until the pale morning came at last.
